title: per-request token (multi-tenancy backbone)
description: Replace the static api_token with a per-request one via a contextvar. cognee_client.py: _get_headers() reads from a contextvar; server.py: middleware in run_http_with_cors extracts Authorization: Bearer -> contextvar. Keep in a separate module/commit for upstream PR-1. SPEC.md sec.6 items 1-2, sec.4.2-4.3.
acceptance_criteria: One API-mode instance serves 2 JWTs -> 2 cognee-API users -> separate datasets.

title: built-in OAuth 2.1 AS (replaces mcp-auth-proxy)
description: Implement OAuthAuthorizationServerProvider (~10 methods) delegating to Google + a Workspace allowlist. Enable via FastMCP(auth_server_provider=..., auth=AuthSettings(client_registration_options=enabled)). Persist clients/codes/refresh (Postgres), not in-memory. Separate module/commit for upstream PR-2. SPEC.md sec.4.4, sec.6 item 3.
acceptance_criteria: Claude.ai connects directly (DCR/PKCE); mcp-auth-proxy removed from the pod.

title: identity bridge (Google user -> cognee auth)
description: Decide option A (per-user api-key) vs B (custom fastapi_users JWT backend in cognee trusting an RS256 JWT with sub=email). B is cleaner but patches cognee. SPEC.md sec.4.6, sec.6 item 4, sec.8 q.1.

title: multi-tenant fork + built-in OAuth 2.1
description: One MCP instance -> N users (own graph per user) + the server itself as an OAuth 2.1 AS for Claude.ai, without mcp-auth-proxy and without a wrapper. Result: one container instead of three. See SPEC.md.
design: mcp==1.27.0 (native OAuth: auth_server_provider/AuthSettings/DCR). Multi-user backbone exists (API mode). Blocker: static api_token (server.py:1942) -> per-request via contextvar. cognee-API trusts only real JWT/api-key.

title: single-container deploy + upstream PR strategy
description: Build the fork image, update the pod kube manifest (drop mcp-auth-proxy, repoint the reverse proxy to the MCP port). E2E with two accounts -> separate graphs. Upstream: PR-1 (per-request), after merge PR-2 (OAuth). SPEC.md sec.7, sec.9.
